clusters.root_cause_summary: Both scenes were reliably solved by baseline/early candidates until a later patch invalidated the working trajectory: candidate_2's hysteresis patch disrupted mL8ThkuaVTM's passive climb_stair path (triggering a get_close_to_stair↔look_for_downstair oscillation), and candidate_35's RSD discount suppressed p53SfW6mjZe's TV-containing frontier 19 steps early, locking the agent at DENSITY_FLOOR for 320+ remaining steps.
clusters.cross_scene_pattern: Identical pattern: a working navigation path existed before a patch (mL8ThkuaVTM: candidate_0 SUCCESS in 312 steps via passive climb_stair; p53SfW6mjZe: 12/13 candidates SUCCESS in ~121 steps). The patch modified a shared code path without protecting the existing high-value trajectory. Different sub-mechanisms: mL8ThkuaVTM's regression is stair-mode oscillation (reinits inflated 3→6, budget exhausted at step 499), while p53SfW6mjZe's is frontier score suppression (DENSITY_FLOOR lock-in, false-positive STOP at step 499). Both produce full-budget failures despite the goal being reachable.
clusters.transfer_notes: Both regressions confirm the same anti-pattern: patches that modify shared scoring or mode-entry logic must preserve existing high-value trajectories. mL8ThkuaVTM teaches that ANY stair mode-entry patch must explicitly preserve the passive climb_stair detection path (the one that worked in candidate_0). p53SfW6mjZe teaches that ANY frontier scoring discount must include a raw-signal floor — frontiers above a BLIP-2 threshold (e.g., 0.10) must be immune to density penalties regardless of zone saturation. These are independent fixes but share the implementation discipline of backward-compatibility guards.
clusters.highest_leverage_untested: Two independent high-confidence fixes: (1) revert_hysteresis_patch for mL8ThkuaVTM — candidate_0 already proves the scene is solvable without it; preserve_passive_climb_stair_detection_during_explore is the exact lever needed. (2) RSD_score_floor_protection for p53SfW6mjZe — never discount a frontier whose raw BLIP-2 score exceeds 0.10; the TV frontier at step 78 had a raw score of 0.107 which RSD reduced to 0.021, so a threshold of 0.10 would have preserved it exactly.

clusters.root_cause_summary: The stair centroid(s) lie in a navmesh-disconnected component relative to the agent: every Reach_stair_centroid attempt returns False, floor_step never resets to 0, and the goal floor is never entered. All tested DP and code-level interventions (12+ mechanisms across 35 candidates) produce identical behavioral fingerprints.
clusters.cross_scene_pattern: Identical: repeated Reach_stair_centroid=False, floor never changes, all candidates produce the same behavioral fingerprint (steps, reinit count, mode sequence). Different: q3zU7Yy5E5s targets upstairs with a single persistent centroid (35 consecutive fails); qyAac8rV8Zk targets downstairs with a two-disable cycle (RSD shifted centroid by ~0.04m in candidate_35 but both original and shifted positions are equally disconnected). The disconnection is area-wide in both scenes, not centroid-specific.
clusters.transfer_notes: mode_attempt_registry (candidate_13) proved that blocking stair re-entry after T failures has zero effect: the agent re-enables the stair via floor reinit ('Navigating upstairs/downstairs to unexplored floor') before the registry can act, so the fix must intercept centroid selection BEFORE get_close_to_stair is entered, not during or after repeated failures inside it. RSD (candidate_35) on qyAac8rV8Zk confirmed the disconnection is area-wide — a 0.04m centroid shift still lands in the same disconnected component, ruling out any frontier-scoring approach to this problem.
clusters.highest_leverage_untested: habitat_navmesh_island_radius_query_before_get_close_to_stair: before entering get_close_to_stair, call sim.pathfinder.island_radius(snapped_centroid) or check is_navigable with tight tolerance to verify the stair centroid shares a connected navmesh island with the agent. If disconnected, skip the centroid entirely and fall through to passive_floor_detection (triggering climb_stair without the get_close_to_stair prerequisite) as an alternative floor transition path. This addresses the root cause at selection time rather than reacting to repeated Reach_stair_centroid=False failures.

clusters.root_cause_summary: The stair is physically traversed (floor_step resets to 0 at step 198, floor 2 entered at step 199), but the destination floor presents only 2 frontiers within a navmesh-disconnected component that excludes the bed room; both frontiers are exhausted within 16 floor_steps and the episode terminates with no remaining frontiers, stairs, or recovery path.
clusters.cross_scene_pattern: Single scene. Structurally distinct from the pre-climb navmesh cluster: the agent successfully crosses the stair, making all stair-entry-level interventions irrelevant. All 14 tested candidates produce identical post-climb behavior (same 2 frontiers, same 16-step exhaustion, same reinit message 'In all floors, no unexplored stairs or frontiers found, stopping.'). Three T4_NOQUIT rescues all find an empty frontier pool, confirming the failure is in frontier generation for the disconnected floor component, not in episode termination logic.
clusters.transfer_notes: The pre-climb navmesh cluster (q3zU7Yy5E5s/qyAac8rV8Zk) demonstrates that sim.pathfinder.island_radius can distinguish connected from disconnected navmesh components. The same API applied post-climb — checking whether floor-2 frontiers share a connected component with the stair landing — could detect the disconnection immediately at step 199 rather than requiring 16 steps to exhaust the frontier pool. candidate_35's RSD active on floor 2 (0.9m frontier score reduced 0.107→0.021) confirms scoring changes are orthogonal: the issue is frontier generation coverage, not frontier ranking.
clusters.highest_leverage_untested: frontier_seeding_beyond_stair_landing_via_3d_raycast_or_room_boundary_detection: after entering a new floor, if the frontier pool is ≤2 and all frontiers are within 1.0m of the stair landing, cast rays from the landing into adjacent unoccupied volume (or sample navigable points within a 3–5m radius using sim.pathfinder.get_random_navigable_point_near) to seed synthetic waypoints in the disconnected room. A lower-implementation-cost alternative is downstairs_fallback_to_retry_different_floor_entry_point_after_floor2_exhaustion: after reinit fires with empty frontiers on floor 2, re-enter floor 1 via a different stair landing offset and attempt floor 2 re-entry from a new position.

forbidden_moves.reason: Mandatory forbidden move per problem statement; appears in ruled_out_levers for all five scenes across all clusters.
Ruled out for both q3zU7Yy5E5s and qyAac8rV8Zk; additionally caused the mL8ThkuaVTM regression in Cluster patch_regression_solvable_episode — this patch is net-negative across the search set.

Candidate_35 confirmed RSD is orthogonal to navmesh disconnection: it shifted centroid selection by 0.04m in qyAac8rV8Zk but both original and shifted centroids remain in the disconnected component. RSD also caused the p53SfW6mjZe regression in Cluster patch_regression_solvable_episode.

Candidate_13 proved behavioral no-op for both cluster members: the stair is re-enabled by floor reinit logic after each blacklisting, so the registry cannot prevent re-entry. The fix must act at centroid selection time, before get_close_to_stair is entered.

Ruled out for both q3zU7Yy5E5s and qyAac8rV8Zk: a time-limited look_for_downstair budget does not prevent re-entry because floor reinit re-enables the stair before look_for_downstair is even reached — the agent cycles through get_close_to_stair, not look_for_downstair, when the centroid is disconnected.

Explicitly ruled out for both cluster members: the gate checks feasibility at look_for_downstair entry, but the actual failure occurs in get_close_to_stair (Reach_stair_centroid=False). The gate never fires because the agent never reaches look_for_downstair on the first stair attempt — it stalls and disables at the get_close_to_stair stage.

Ruled out for both cluster members: the disconnection is area-wide, not centroid-specific. Blacklisting one centroid causes selection of an adjacent centroid that is equally unreachable, as demonstrated by RSD's 0.04m centroid shift in qyAac8rV8Zk producing identical Reach_stair_centroid=False outcomes.

Caused the p53SfW6mjZe regression in this cluster: RSD must not be applied without a score-floor protection gate (raw BLIP-2 > 0.10). Reapplying RSD in its candidate_35 form on any previously-solvable single-floor episode is a forbidden regression risk.

Caused the mL8ThkuaVTM regression in this cluster: the patch diverted the agent from passive climb_stair into an oscillating get_close_to_stair↔look_for_downstair cycle that exhausted the step budget. Re-applying any variant that modifies stair mode-entry logic without explicitly preserving passive climb_stair detection is forbidden.

Candidate_35 confirmed RSD is orthogonal to post-climb frontier exhaustion: RSD was active on floor 2 (0.9m frontier discounted 0.107→0.021) but the episode still terminated identically — the issue is frontier generation coverage, not frontier ranking, making any scoring change a no-op.
